concepts.title: Congress Dominance
concepts.description: The Congress party dominated Indian politics from 1947 to 1967, with Jawaharlal Nehru as the first Prime Minister, followed by Lal Bahadur Shastri and Indira Gandhi, marking a significant era in Indian political history.

concepts.title: Congress System
concepts.description: The Congress system refers to the political arrangement where the Congress party was the central element and other parties were marginal, as stated in the NCERT textbook, highlighting the party's significant role in shaping India's political landscape.

concepts.title: Indira Gandhi's Rule
concepts.description: Indira Gandhi's rule is a crucial topic, with her imposition of the Emergency in 1975 being a significant event, leading to the erosion of the Congress system and the rise of opposition parties, often asked in CBSE exams.

concepts.title: Non-Congress Governments
concepts.description: To remember the first non-Congress governments in Indian states, use the mnemonic 'SPB' - Samyukta Pradesh (UP), Punjab, and Bihar, which formed non-Congress governments in the 1960s, marking a shift in Indian politics.

concepts.title: Congress System vs. One-Party Dominance
concepts.description: Students often confuse the Congress system with one-party dominance; however, the Congress system refers to the party's central role, while one-party dominance refers to the party's electoral superiority, a distinction that must be clearly understood to avoid errors.

concepts.title: 1967 Elections
concepts.description: The 1967 elections are crucial to remember, as they marked a significant turning point in Indian politics, with non-Congress parties forming governments in several states, including UP, Bihar, and Punjab, leading to the decline of the Congress system.

concepts.title: National Emergency
concepts.description: The National Emergency imposed by Indira Gandhi in 1975 is a vital topic, leading to widespread protests, the rise of opposition parties, and ultimately, the defeat of the Congress party in the 1977 general elections, a pivotal moment in Indian political history.

concepts.title: Opposition Parties
concepts.description: The NCERT textbook highlights the growth of opposition parties, including the Swatantra Party, the Jana Sangh, and the Communist Party of India, which posed a challenge to the Congress system, eventually leading to its decline, an essential concept to understand.
